payload.name: Broadcom Inc.
payload.narrative: Broadcom Inc. (AVGO) designs and manufactures a diversified portfolio of semiconductor and infrastructure software products, with core franchises in custom AI accelerators (XPUs/ASICs for hyperscalers like Google), Ethernet networking silicon (estimated >70% share in AI networking chips), and broadband/RF components. Following the $69 billion VMware acquisition completed in late 2023, its Infrastructure Software segment has expanded into a significant revenue driver through subscription-model enterprise software, making the company a dual-engine supplier spanning both the hardware and software layers of AI data center build-outs. AVGO is on the watchlist as a chokepoint position in the AI supply chain — its custom silicon and networking franchises represent concentrated, hard-to-displace exposure to hyperscaler AI infrastructure spending, while the VMware integration introduces a software-recurring-revenue dimension worth monitoring for margin trajectory and customer retention dynamics.
catalysts: Quarterly earnings and forward guidance cadence
Sector-specific demand cycle and supply-chain signals
Macro regime shifts affecting risk appetite
